# emma_volume_configurations (Data Source)

This data source retrieves available volume configurations for a specific data center in the Emma platform.

Use this data source to discover valid volume types, size ranges, and pricing information when planning volume deployments.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Query available volume configurations for a data center
data "emma_data_center" "aws_us_east" {
  name = "AWS US East 1"
}

data "emma_volume_configurations" "aws_configs" {
  data_center_id = data.emma_data_center.aws_us_east.id
}

# Display available volume types and pricing
output "available_volume_types" {
  value = [for config in data.emma_volume_configurations.aws_configs.configurations : config.volume_type]
}

output "volume_pricing" {
  value = {
    for config in data.emma_volume_configurations.aws_configs.configurations :
    "${config.volume_type}_${config.volume_gb}gb" => config.price_per_month
  }
}

# Use configuration data to create a volume with valid parameters
resource "emma_volume" "data_volume" {
  data_center_id = data.emma_data_center.aws_us_east.id
  volume_gb      = data.emma_volume_configurations.aws_configs.configurations[0].volume_gb
  volume_type    = data.emma_volume_configurations.aws_configs.configurations[0].volume_type
  name           = "my-data-volume"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `data_center_id` (String) ID of the data center to query volume configurations for

### Read-Only

- `configurations` (Attributes List) List of available volume configurations (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--configurations))

<a id="nestedatt--configurations"></a>
### Nested Schema for `configurations`

Read-Only:

- `data_center_id` (String) ID of the data center
- `data_center_name` (String) Name of the data center
- `location_id` (Number) Location ID
- `location_name` (String) Location name (city or state)
- `price_per_month` (Number) Price per month for this configuration
- `provider_id` (Number) ID of the cloud provider
- `provider_name` (String) Name of the cloud provider
- `volume_gb` (Number) Volume size in gigabytes
- `volume_type` (String) Volume type (e.g., ssd, hdd)

## Notes

- This data source is useful for discovering available volume options before creating volumes
- Configurations include pricing information to help with cost planning
- Each configuration represents a valid combination of volume type and size for the specified data center
- Use this data source to ensure your volume resource configurations use valid parameters
